meaning: adj.[生]分裂生殖的,有分裂能力的
examples: It is impossible to fix the north Caucasus, or indeed any of the country's fissiparous regions, without dealing with Moscow's own larger defects.
Russia's far east has always been the most strategically vulnerable part of Moscow's fissiparous imperium, in what is the world's biggest country.
Belmoktar remains associated with this fissiparous group -- but is very much his own man.
But America is fissiparous enough without widening the cracks by insensitive public behaviour.
Yet even as the new money was giving a shine to the country's ailing finances, cracks were appearing in Mr Deuba's own, fissiparous, Nepali Congress party.
